howItWorks.intro: This one rep max calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Individual results vary based on metabolism, genetics, and activity level. Consult your doctor before starting any program.

faq.question: What is 1RM?
faq.answer: The maximum weight you can lift for a single repetition.

faq.question: How accurate is this?
faq.answer: Most accurate with 3-5 rep sets. Less accurate above 10 reps.

faq.question: Is the One Rep Max Strength Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard fitness and training formulas trusted by athletes and fitness enthusiasts. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What weight lifted should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate weight lifted value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the One Rep Max Strength Calculator?
sections.body: The One Rep Max Strength Calculator is a free online tool designed for athletes and fitness enthusiasts who need quick, accurate calculations in the fitness and training space. By entering your weight lifted, reps performed, you get instant results including estimated 1rm, 90%, 80%. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting estimated 1rm right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In fitness and training, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by athletes and fitness enthusiasts worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to optimize your training with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your weight lifted and need to find the right estimated 1rm. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Weight Lifted (lbs)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your weight lifted in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Reps Performed
howToSteps.description: Type or select your reps performed in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your estimated 1rm, 90%, 80%. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your weight lifted before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both estimated 1rm and 90% — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to optimize your training.
If you're unsure about your reps performed, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
